title: unicrystalline
intro: The unicrystalline rank in plant taxonomy refers to a type of plant that produces a single crystal or stone within its cells, such as calcified cells or raphides. This characteristic is often found in plants with specialized cell walls or structures that provide additional support and protection. Examples of unicrystalline orders include Asparagales, Malpighiales, and Poales, which include a diverse range of plants from grasses and sedges to orchids and tropical trees. The unicrystalline rank highlights the unique adaptations of certain plant groups to their environments and the importance of crystal formation in plant cell structure and function.

orders.order_desc: Capparales is an order of flowering plants in the clade Rosidae, belonging to the class Magnoliopsida.
